revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24167314344|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:筑後川花火大会]] {{Source Citation| date = November 2022|qq=}} {{Fireworks Festival | Name = Chikugo River Fireworks Festival | English name = | image = File:ColorfulFireworks.png | Image description = | Alias = | Official name = | Former name = {{ill|Suitengu Dedication Fireworks Festival|ja|水天宮奉納花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Dedication Fireworks Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135551185}}
（{{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}}まで） | Event Date = {{ill|August 5|en|August 5|qid=Q2783|lt=August 5|1=August 5}} | First Held = {{ill|1650|en|1650|qid=Q6951|lt=1650|1=1650}} | Venue = Kyomachi, Shino-yama | Number of Fireworks = 18,000 | Organizer = Chikugo River Fireworks Festival Executive Committee | Sponsor = | Cooperation = | Operation = | Fireworks Handling = | Nearest Station = {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=JR|1=Kyushu Railway Company}}{{ill|Kurume Station|en|Kurume Station|qid=Q1122628|lt=Kurume Station|1=Kurume Station}} | Direct Bus = From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qid=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}
temporary shuttle bus in operation | URL = http://kurume-matsuri.info/fire/index.html | External Link Name = Kurume City Homepage | Special Notes = }} '''Chikugo River Fireworks Festival''' (筑後川花火大会) is a {{ill|Fireworks Festival|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=Fireworks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q127933}} held every August in {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume City|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. == Overview == It attracts over 450,000 spectators every year, and the riverside of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}} – the largest river in {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|qid=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}} – serves as the venue. Approximately 18,000 {{ill|Fireworks|en|Fireworks|qid=Q127933|lt=Fireworks|1=Fireworks}} are launched annually, making it one of the largest in {{ill|Western Japan|ja|西日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Western Japan|1=Western Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1063946}}, and it is the only major fireworks festival held solely in Fukuoka Prefecture with over 10,000 launches (the {{ill|Kanmon Straits Fireworks Festival|ja|関門海峡花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmon Straits Fireworks Festival|1=Kanmon Straits Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11656483}}, with about 15,000 launches, is co-hosted with Yamaguchi Prefecture). Excellent star mines with a well-structured ({{ill|Kishōtenketsu|en|Kishōtenketsu|qid=Q1631904|lt=Kishotenten|1=Kishōtenketsu}}) design, large-scale mechanized fireworks including the Niagara, and giant shell fireworks burst forth like enormous blossoms in the night sky. The event is bustling with many couples and families dressed in {{ill|Yukata|en|Yukata|qid=Q849302|lt=Yukata|1=Yukata}}, while over 400 {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|qid=Q16556730|lt=Stall|1=Market stall}} line the venue, and vendors offering Kurume {{ill|B-class Gourmet|ja|B級グルメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/B-class Gourmet|1=B-class gourmet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10854459}} also participate. The Chikugo River Fireworks Festival is an {{ill|Event|ja|イベント|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Event|1=event|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1656682}} presented as the dedicated fireworks for the Suitengu Summer Festival, and historically it is unrelated to the “Kurume Water Festival.” == History == It originated in {{ill|1650|en|1650|qid=Q6951|lt=1650|1=1650}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|qid=Q1146135|lt=Keian|1=Keian}}) when the lord of the {{ill|Kurume Domain|en|Kurume Domain|qid=Q426826|lt=Kurume Domain|1=Kurume Domain}}, {{ill|Arima Tadayori|ja|有馬忠頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arima Tadayori|1=Arima Tadayori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516786}}, donated a shrine building to the {{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|qid=Q3200625|lt=Suitengu|1=Kurume Suitengū}} site and dedicated fireworks to celebrate its completion. Subsequently, it was held as the “Suitengu Dedication Fireworks Festival” and, after nine cancellations due to turmoil and wars, adopted its current name in {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}). Furthermore, with the integration of various city festivals into the Kurume Water Festival starting in {{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Showa 47), the event also came to signify the finale of that festival. Originally held solely on the riverside in front of Suitengu, following the {{ill|Akashi pedestrian bridge accident|en|Akashi pedestrian bridge accident|qid=Q4700349|lt=Akashi Fireworks Pedestrian Bridge Accident|1=Akashi pedestrian bridge accident}} in {{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (the 13th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}), from {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}} (Heisei 16) the then-{{ill|Kurume Police Station|ja|久留米警察署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume Police Station|1=Kurume Police Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369543}} {{ill|Police Chief|ja|警察署長|lt=Chief|lt_ja=長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Police Chief|1=Police Chief|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210737}} initiated a split launch with the Shino-yama Venue located on the Kurume Castle riverside. Accordingly, the original venue was renamed “Kyomachi Venue.” Furthermore, from {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17), {{ill|Tosu, Saga|en|Tosu, Saga|qid=Q860674|lt=Tosu City|1=Tosu, Saga}} joined as a co-host, establishing a viewing area at the sports field in Shimonochō on the border of Kurume City as the “Tosu Venue.” In {{ill|2019|en|2019|qid=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}} (the first year of Reiwa), the Kurume City Violence Expulsion Promotion Council intensified efforts to exclude {{ill|Yakuza|ja|暴力団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuza|1=Japanese organized crime group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11090465}} from the {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|qid=Q16556730|lt=Stall|1=Market stall}} vendors participating in the festival, instituting a rule that all stalls must display the “Violence Expulsion Declaration Plate” distributed by the city. In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qid=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} (Reiwa 2) and the following {{ill|2021|en|2021|qid=Q49628|lt=2021|1=2021}} (Reiwa 3), the event was cancelled due to the {{ill|COVID-19 Outbreak in Japan|ja|日本における2019th year コロナウイルス感染症の流行状況|lt=COVID-19 Outbreak|lt_ja=新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 Outbreak in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|2022|en|2022|qid=Q49625|lt=2022|1=2022}} (Reiwa 4), the festival was held for the first time in three years; however, due to movement restrictions during the COVID-19 outbreak, the number of launches was reduced to approximately 8,000 and the launch duration to 30 minutes, significantly downsizing the event.[http://www.city.kurume.fukuoka.jp/1500soshiki/9119kanko/3010oshirase/2022-0707-1856-289.html ] Additionally, during the event, an accident occurred in which a firework shell fell and exploded at the archery field of the {{ill|Kurume National College of Technology|ja|久留米工業高等専門学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume National College of Technology|1=Kurume National College of Technology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369521}}, located approximately 260 meters from the launch site.{{Cite web|和書|title=弓道場に花火玉が落下した事故、製造や打ち上げ時のミスが原因か…調査報告書 |url=https://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/20221121-OYT1T50251/ |website=読売新聞オンライン |date=2022-11-21 |access-date=2022-11-21 |language=ja|qq=}} The accident was attributed to a combination of human errors and other factors. In {{ill|2023|en|2023|qid=Q49622|lt=2023|1=2023}} (Reiwa 5), the event date was changed to August 27, and if inclement weather prevents the event from taking place on that day, it will be cancelled rather than postponed. The Tosu Venue was not set up, the number of launches was set at 15,000, and the launch duration at 70 minutes (with the end time advanced to 20:50), marking a reduction in scale compared to prior to {{ill|2019|en|2019|qid=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nishinippon.co.jp/item/n/1102583/ |title=筑後川花火大会、今年は8月27日開催　福岡・久留米市、1万5千発規模に |access-date=2023-06-30 |publisher=西日本新聞me|qq=}} == Date and Time == : It is held on {{ill|August 5|en|August 5|qid=Q2783|lt=August 5|1=August 5}} every year regardless of the day of the week. In case of rain, it is postponed on alternate days to {{ill|August 7|en|August 7|qid=Q2781|lt=7th|1=August 7}}, {{ill|August 9|en|August 9|qid=Q2782|lt=9th|1=August 9}}, and {{ill|August 11|en|August 11|qid=Q2778|lt=11th|1=August 11}}. : Typically, fireworks are launched from 19:40 to 21:10. ※ Until 2019 == Venue == Six venues are provided as fireworks viewing locations. At the Shino-yama Venue, shell fireworks begin to be launched around 20:15. Since the riverside of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}} is spacious, it is possible to walk between venues (the Kyomachi Venue and the Shino-yama Venue). In suburban areas without obstacles such as buildings, one can watch the fireworks standing; however, the overwhelming impact as experienced at the venue is diminished. === Viewing Locations === All venues are located on the riverside of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}}, and unless otherwise noted, are situated in {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume City|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. * Kyomachi Venue – Riverside on the left bank in front of Suitengu from Nisenmanbashi to {{ill|Nagatoishi Bridge|ja|長門石橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatoishi Bridge|1=Nagatoishi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654894}} ** Nagatoishi Venue – Right bank riverside around {{ill|Nagatoishi Bridge|ja|長門石橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatoishi Bridge|1=Nagatoishi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654894}} ** Miyaki Venue ({{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}{{ill|Miyaki, Saga|en|Miyaki, Saga|qid=Q1351707|lt=Miyaki Town|1=Miyaki, Saga}}) – Right bank riverside upstream of {{ill|Mamezu Bridge|ja|豆津橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamezu Bridge|1=Mamezu Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633152}} * Shino-yama Venue – Riverside on the left bank in front of Kurume Castle from {{ill|Nagatoishi Bridge|ja|長門石橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatoishi Bridge|1=Nagatoishi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654894}} to {{ill|Mamezu Bridge|ja|豆津橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamezu Bridge|1=Mamezu Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633152}} ** Komorino Venue – Right bank riverside from {{ill|Komorino Bridge|ja|小森野橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komorino Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Nisenmanbashi ** Tosu Venue ({{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}{{ill|Tosu, Saga|en|Tosu, Saga|qid=Q860674|lt=Tosu City|1=Tosu, Saga}}) – Right bank at Shimonochō Sports Field ※ Not set up in {{ill|2023|en|2023|qid=Q49622|lt=2023|1=2023}} === Launch Locations === * Across from Kyomachi Venue * Across from Shino-yama Venue == Traffic Restrictions == Vehicular traffic is prohibited primarily on the embankment road between {{ill|Kurume Ohashi|ja|久留米大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume Ohashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mamezu Bridge|ja|豆津橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamezu Bridge|1=Mamezu Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633152}}, as well as along both banks of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}}. In all cases, restrictions are enforced from 18:30 to 22:00 (with some sections until 22:30). ; No Entry * Embankment road along the right bank of the Chikugo River behind {{ill|Kurume National College of Technology|ja|久留米工業高等専門学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume National College of Technology|1=Kurume National College of Technology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369521}} and along the left bank of the {{ill|Homan River|ja|宝満川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homan River|1=Hōman River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452606}} (launch location for Shino-yama Venue) * A section of the embankment road along the right bank of the Chikugo River between {{ill|Nagatoishi Bridge|ja|長門石橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatoishi Bridge|1=Nagatoishi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654894}} and {{ill|Mamezu Bridge|ja|豆津橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamezu Bridge|1=Mamezu Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633152}} (launch location for Kyomachi Venue) * {{ill|Komorino Bridge|ja|小森野橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komorino Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Downstream sidewalk of {{ill|Nagatoishi Bridge|ja|長門石橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatoishi Bridge|1=Nagatoishi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654894}} ; Road Closed to Vehicles * All roads on the left bank riverside of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}} from the {{ill|Mamezu Bridge|ja|豆津橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamezu Bridge|1=Mamezu Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633152}} Intersection – Suitengu Entrance Intersection – Nawa-te Intersection – Keyaki Street – Kushihara Bypass – East Intersection of the Fire Department Headquarters – from the south end of Nisenmanbashi ** {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture Road 145 Eguchi Nagatoishi Eshima Line|ja|佐賀県道・福岡県道145号江口長門石江島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture Road 145 Eguchi Nagatoishi Eshima Line|1=Saga Prefectural Road and Fukuoka Prefectural Road Route 145|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11384863}} and {{ill|Nagatoishi Bridge|ja|長門石橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatoishi Bridge|1=Nagatoishi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654894}} are exempt from restrictions ** From Asahimachi Intersection on I-Dai Street to in front of the University Hospital serves as a shuttle bus waiting area * Embankment road on both banks between {{ill|Kurume Ohashi|ja|久留米大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume Ohashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Komorino Bridge|ja|小森野橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komorino Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Embankment road along the right bank of the Anra River, {{ill|Homan River|ja|宝満川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homan River|1=Hōman River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452606}}, and the Chikugo River from {{ill|Torinan Bridge|ja|鳥南橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinan Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Mamezu Bridge|ja|豆津橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamezu Bridge|1=Mamezu Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633152}} * Embankment road on the west side of {{ill|Fukuoka/Saga Prefecture Road 17 Kurume Motoyama Chikushino Line|ja|福岡県道・佐賀県道17号久留米基山筑紫野線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka/Saga Prefecture Road 17 Kurume Motoyama Chikushino Line|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Road and Saga Prefectural Road Route 17|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592901}} * Road from Shimonochō Intersection to the riverside of the Chikugo River * Road from the intersection in front of {{ill|Hizen-Asahi Station|en|Hizen-Asahi Station|qid=Q5359363|lt=Hizen Asahi Station|1=Hizen-Asahi Station}} to the riverside of the Chikugo River == Access == === Rail and Bus === * Kyomachi Venue – Walk from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=JR|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kurume Station|en|Kurume Station|qid=Q1122628|lt=Kurume Station|1=Kurume Station}} * Shino-yama Venue – Take a temporary bus from {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qid=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}} and alight at the University Hospital bus stop ({{ill|Kurume University Hospital|ja|久留米大学病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume University Hospital|1=Kurume University Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369514}}) * Komorino Venue – 20-minute walk from {{ill|Miyanojin Station|en|Miyanojin Station|qid=Q6884381|lt=Miyanojin Station|1=Miyanojin Station}} * Tosu Venue – 30-minute walk from {{ill|Hizen-Asahi Station|en|Hizen-Asahi Station|qid=Q5359363|lt=Hizen Asahi Station|1=Hizen-Asahi Station}} * Nagatoishi Venue – Alight at the Welfare Center bus stop on Nishitetsu Bus route 40 * Miyaki Venue – Alight at the Mamezu Bus Stop on Nishitetsu Bus routes 41 or 45 On the day of the event, temporary trains will operate on JR and Nishitetsu lines. The shuttle bus runs only between {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qid=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}} and the University Hospital bus stop near Shino-yama Venue ({{ill|Kurume University Hospital|ja|久留米大学病院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume University Hospital|1=Kurume University Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369514}}). As all venues are within approximately 2 km of Kurume Station, walking between them is also feasible. === Private Car === Due to traffic restrictions and congestion on the day of the event, it is difficult to enter central Kurume City by private car, and no parking is provided; therefore, the use of public transportation is strongly encouraged (in the past, the parking lot of {{ill|Ekimae Real Estate Stadium|en|Ekimae Real Estate Stadium|qid=Q1060615|lt=Front Station Real Estate Stadium|1=Ekimae Real Estate Stadium}}, adjacent to {{ill|Tosu Station|en|Tosu Station|qid=Q840544|lt=Tosu Station|1=Tosu Station}}, was opened on the day of the event, but this has now been discontinued). == Footnotes == {{reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.suitengu.net National Main Shrine {{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|qid=Q125319456|lt=Suitengu|1=Suitengū}}] * [http://www.kurume-matsuri.info/firemap.html Viewing Locations of the Chikugo River Fireworks Festival] * [http://www.kurume-matsuri.info/fire/index.html Official Website of the Kurume Water Festival] {{Event-stub|qq=}} {{Myōken Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|筑後川花火大会|version=100126263|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:筑後川花火大会]] {{wikidata link|Q11602813}} [[Category:Japan in 1650]] [[Category:August]] [[Category:Culture of Kurume]] [[Category:久留米藩]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:福岡県の花火大会]] [[Category:筑後川水系]] [[Category:西日本新聞社]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
